batch.deprecated

Class BatchConsole

  • java.lang.Object
    • batch.deprecated.BatchExecutor
      • batch.deprecated.BatchConsole
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.lang.Iterable<MediaFile>

    public final class BatchConsole
extends BatchExecutor
    The primary Command Line Interface (CLI) entry point for the batch processing engine.

    This class provides a terminal-based implementation of the BatchExecutor, allowing users to interact with the engine via a console environment. It manages the execution lifecycle, from parsing input parameters to displaying real-time progress updates to the user.

    Processes a collection of media files by copying them from a source directory to a target destination. Files are renamed using a configurable prefix and chronological index, and can be sorted in ascending or descending order based on their original Date Taken metadata.

    This executor performs "surgical" binary patching on the copied files to align internal metadata (EXIF, XMP, etc.) with specified timestamps and synchronises the operating system's file-system attributes (Creation, Last Modified, and Last Access times).

    Since:
    9 February 2026
    Version:
    1.2
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

      • LOGGER

        private static final logger.LogFactory LOGGER

      • DF

        private static final java.text.SimpleDateFormat DF

    • Constructor Detail

      • BatchConsole

        protected BatchConsole(BatchConfiguration config)
                throws BatchErrorException
        Constructs a console interface using a BatchConfiguration configuration.

        This constructor is invoked via BatchBuilder.build() to ensure all configuration constraints are validated before instantiation.

        Parameters:
        config - the immutable configuration object containing the validated parameters required to execute the batch
        Throws:
        BatchErrorException - if any metadata-related reading error occurs during initialisation

    • Method Detail

      • processBatchCopy

        protected void processBatchCopy()
        Executes the sequential copying and metadata patching process.

        Iterates through the sorted set of MediaFile objects, performing the following steps for each:

        • Generates a new filename based on prefix, index, and optional time-stamp
        • Copies the file to the target directory while preserving original attributes
        • If forced or metadata is missing, patches internal binary date tags (EXIF/XMP)
        • Synchronises OS-level time-stamps with the media's capture time
        Specified by:
        processBatchCopy in class BatchExecutor

      • generateTargetName

        private java.lang.String generateTargetName(MediaFile media,
                                            int index)
        Generates a target file name based on the media type.

        If the media is a video, the original filename is returned in lowercase. If the media is an image, the name is constructed as: {prefix}{index}{timestamp}.{extension}

        Parameters:
        media - the MediaFile object to rename
        index - a sequence number for ordering
        Returns:
        the formatted target file name

      • scanArguments

        private static cli.CommandFlagParser scanArguments(java.lang.String[] arguments)
        Configures and parses the supported command-line arguments.

        This method defines the rules for known flags and options. Note that most option handling logic is delegated to the surrounding Builder-pattern implementation, while this method primarily registers the supported flags.

        Parameters:
        arguments - the raw command-line arguments passed to main
        Returns:
        a CommandFlagParser instance, already configured and parsed for the current invocation

      • showUsage

        private static void showUsage()
        Prints the command usage line, showing the correct flag options.

      • showHelp

        private static void showHelp()
        Prints detailed usage help information, providing guidance on how to use this program.

      • execute

        private static void execute(java.lang.String[] arguments)
        Begins the execution process by reading arguments from the command line and processing them.
        Parameters:
        arguments - an array of strings containing the command line arguments

      • main

        public static void main(java.lang.String[] args)
